title: lidr-models prediction artifact
description: Schema for a versioned JSON artifact produced by one model and consumed by lidr. v2 adds model_id/model_version (multi-model identity) and a per-prediction recommendation field.

schema_version.description: Major schema version. Bumped on any breaking change (field removal/rename/type change). Additive optional fields do NOT require a bump.

model_id.description: Stable identifier for the producing model family (e.g. 'ta_ensemble', 'news_sentiment').

model_version.description: Version of this model's code+config combination. Recommended: semver-ish 'MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH'.

config_name.description: The specific experiment configuration (e.g. 'baseline_six_signals_unweighted'). Distinguishes two runs of the same model_id.

ticker.description: The asset symbol the predictions are for (e.g. 'SPY').

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 timestamp marking when this artifact was produced. Also accepts the legacy 'YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS' stamp format for backward compatibility with v1-style timestamps.

classification.description: Classification metrics over the stitched OOS predictions.

strategy.description: Strategy (long when y_pred=1) metrics on the OOS equity curve.

benchmark.description: Buy-and-hold reference metrics over the same OOS window.

recommendation.description: Human-facing label lidr displays. Derived from probability_up bands today; will come from the 3-class model after that migration. The field exists now so the schema stays stable when that migration lands.

probability_up.description: Calibrated (or in-progress-to-be-calibrated) probability of an up day. The numeric quantity lidr can re-bucket if it disagrees with the model's recommendation bands.

y_pred.description: Hard binary prediction. Today: 1 if model thinks up, 0 if down. Stays in the schema for backtest reproducibility.

y_true.description: Realized outcome (sign of forward return). null on recent dates whose forward window has not yet resolved.
